reqSatisfaction.R1: Controller proves ownership via Ed25519/secp256k1/ML-DSA-44 signature on a challenge. Spec §4.2 defines verification method resolution.
reqSatisfaction.R2: Controller creates DID without central authority — PDA derived from domain hash on Solana. No registration needed beyond an on-chain transaction.
reqSatisfaction.R3: Registry enforces global uniqueness via Solana PDA derivation — same domain hash always maps to same account, collisions impossible.
reqSatisfaction.R4: Resolution reads from public Solana registry and IPFS. No need to contact the issuer — any resolver node can verify independently.
reqSatisfaction.R5: DID Document includes Ed25519, secp256k1, and ML-DSA-44 verification methods. Keys are bound to the DID at creation.
reqSatisfaction.R6: DID Document update operation rotates keys without changing the DID identifier. Spec §5.3 defines the update protocol.
reqSatisfaction.R7: DID Document supports service endpoints for DIDComm v2, credential exchange, and linked domains. Spec §4.4.
reqSatisfaction.R8: No PII stored on-chain. Selective Disclosure via SD-JWT for credential presentations. Domain names are pseudonymous.
reqSatisfaction.R9: Multi-sig controller support — multiple keys can be authorized as controllers with threshold signing.
reqSatisfaction.R10: Works on Solana (global, permissionless). No jurisdiction-specific infrastructure required.
reqSatisfaction.R11: No single authority can revoke the DID. Only the controller's private key can deactivate it.
reqSatisfaction.R12: Minimal Solana rent (~0.002 SOL) for on-chain account. No per-transaction fees for resolution.
reqSatisfaction.R13: Open-source spec and tooling. Standard DID Document format. Universal Resolver driver available.
reqSatisfaction.R14: SD-JWT selective disclosure limits data exposure. Pairwise DIDs possible for privacy-sensitive contexts.
reqSatisfaction.R15: ML-DSA-44 (CRYSTALS-Dilithium) and ML-KEM-768 post-quantum algorithms supported as verification methods.
reqSatisfaction.R16: Data lives on Solana (decentralized, replicated) + IPFS. Survives even if Attestto ceases to exist.
reqSatisfaction.R17: Not tied to a single software deployment. Any compatible resolver can read the on-chain state.
reqSatisfaction.R18: Controller retains keys independent of any service provider. DID is bound to cryptographic keys, not a business relationship.
reqSatisfaction.R19: DIDComm v2 encrypted messaging channel. End-to-end encryption with authenticated key agreement.
reqSatisfaction.R20: Primary registry is Solana, but the method spec allows alternate registries implementing the same PDA derivation interface.
reqSatisfaction.R21: DID binds to legal identity via vLEI verifiable credential. Supports eIDAS and FATF compliance frameworks.
reqSatisfaction.R22: Human-readable SNS domain names (e.g. alice.sol) resolve to DID Documents. No need to memorize cryptographic hashes.

methods.useCaseSummary: Cross-border identity for regulated finance — stablecoin corridors, KYC/AML credential exchange, and legal entity binding via vLEI. Also supports academic credentials, healthcare consent, and encrypted messaging via DIDComm v2.
